name: OpenAI API Key
description: Creates an API key for the OpenAI platform, granting access to GPT, DALL-E, Whisper, Embeddings, and other OpenAI models via the API. Keys can be scoped to specific projects for isolation and billing attribution.
prereqs: A payment method on file or active credits. API access requires a funded account separate from any ChatGPT subscription.
If using project-scoped keys: at least one project created in the OpenAI dashboard.

steps.title: Open the API Keys page
steps.instruction: Navigate to the OpenAI platform API keys page. Log in if prompted.

steps.title: Select the project (optional but recommended)
steps.instruction: At the top of the dashboard, check which project/organisation is selected. If you want the key scoped to a specific project, use the project switcher in the top-left to select it before creating the key. Project-scoped keys can only access resources within that project and make billing attribution easier.
steps.screenshot_hint: Project selector dropdown in the top-left of the OpenAI dashboard header.

steps.title: Create a new secret key
steps.instruction: Click the '+ Create new secret key' button.
steps.screenshot_hint: Green '+ Create new secret key' button on the API keys page.

steps.title: Configure the key
steps.instruction:
In the creation dialog, fill in:

1. Name: A descriptive name like 'rialu-prod' or 'faire-backend'.
2. Project: Confirm the project scope (or 'Default project' for account-wide access).
3. Permissions: Choose 'All' for full access, or 'Restricted' to limit to specific endpoints (e.g. only Chat Completions, only Embeddings). Restricted keys are safer for single-purpose services.

Click 'Create secret key'.
steps.screenshot_hint: Modal dialog with Name field, Project dropdown, and Permissions radio buttons.

steps.title: Copy the API key
steps.instruction: The key is displayed in a modal. It starts with 'sk-'. Click the copy icon or manually select and copy the entire key string.
steps.warning: The key is shown only once. If you close this dialog without copying, you must create a new key. The old one cannot be revealed again.
steps.screenshot_hint: Modal showing the key value starting with 'sk-' and a copy button.

steps.title: Set usage limits
steps.instruction:
Navigate to Settings > Limits (or Billing > Limits) and configure:

1. Monthly budget: Set a hard cap or notification threshold to avoid surprise bills.
2. Per-key rate limits: If available, set rate limits on the new key to prevent runaway usage.

This is especially important for keys used by automated systems.
steps.screenshot_hint: Limits page showing monthly budget input and usage notification threshold.

steps.title: Test the key
steps.instruction:
Verify the key works by running:

curl https://api.openai.com/v1/models \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <your-key>'

You should see a JSON list of available models. If you get a 401, the key was not copied correctly. If you get a 429, check your billing/credits.

vault_entry.notes: Keys start with sk-. Record which project it is scoped to and what permissions (All or Restricted) were granted. Check expiry policy — OpenAI may enforce key rotation.
